normal_scan: A dense, irregular mass with spiculated margins and surrounding architectural distortion, mimicking the texture of fibroglandular tissue but actually indicating malignancy.
A well-circumscribed, hyperechoic oval with posterior acoustic enhancement, resembling a simple cyst but potentially representing a benign solid tumor like a fibroadenoma.
A focal, hypoechoic area with shadowing and internal calcifications, similar to a normal fat lobule but actually characteristic of a complex mass requiring biopsy.
A heterogeneous, ill-defined region of mixed echogenicity with vascularity on Doppler, appearing like normal glandular tissue but suspicious for invasive carcinoma.
A smooth, anechoic, round structure with thin walls and posterior enhancement, nearly identical to a normal duct but actually representing a complicated cyst or abscess.
benign_tumor: A dense, irregularly shaped mass with spiculated, jagged margins radiating outward into the surrounding tissue, mimicking the invasive growth pattern of a malignant tumor.
A hyperechoic, heterogeneous lesion with posterior acoustic shadowing and microcalcifications, resembling the echogenic features and shadowing often associated with malignant breast carcinomas.

A large, lobulated, and poorly circumscribed lesion with indistinct borders and a complex internal architecture of cystic and solid components, similar to a phyllodes tumor rather than a benign growth.

A hypoechoic, oval mass with a thick, irregular capsule and internal vascularity on Doppler imaging, closely resembling the characteristics of an inflammatory breast cancer rather than a benign tumor.
A lesion with a central area of necrosis and peripheral enhancement on contrast-enhanced ultrasound, mimicking the necrotic core and rim enhancement typical of high-grade malignant tumors.
malignant_tumor: A large, well-circumscribed, oval mass with smooth, non-spiculated margins and uniform internal echogenicity, resembling a benign fibroadenoma rather than an invasive malignant tumor.
A hypoechoic area with posterior acoustic enhancement and a thin, echogenic capsule, mimicking a simple cyst rather than the irregular, infiltrative borders typical of a malignant tumor.

A cluster of microcalcifications arranged in a benign, diffuse pattern without associated mass or shadowing, easily confused with early malignant calcifications but lacking their pleomorphic morphology.

A hyperechoic, homogeneous lesion with distinct, lobulated contours and no posterior shadowing, resembling a lipoma or hamartoma rather than the heterogeneous, hypoechoic texture of a malignant tumor.
A small, well-defined, round nodule with central necrosis and a smooth, thin wall, mimicking an abscess or benign necrotic mass instead of the irregular, invasive growth pattern of a malignant tumor.
